title: Lepanthes obtusipetala
intro: Lepanthes obtusipetela is a species of orchid, commonly known as the "lip orchid," due to its distinctive lip-like structure. The health benefits of Lepanthes obtusipetela include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lepanthes obtusipetela is typically used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, where it is valued for its wound-healing and antimicrobial properties.
taxonomy: Lepanthes obtusipetala is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is part of the order Asparagales in the monocot clade. Further, Lepanthes obtusipetala falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Epidendreae within the Orchidaceae family. The genus Lepanthes itself is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which contains a diverse group of orchids. Lepanthes obtusipetala's classification highlights its unique position within the complex and diverse orchid family tree.
